pr.title: Initial work on matching displayfunc.c, aobj.c, fobj.c, wobj.c
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large early sysdolphin matching PR that moved all of asm/aobj.s into src/sysdolphin/aobj.c and started matching displayfunc.c, fobj.c, and wobj.c. It also added multiple sysdolphin headers and Dolphin GX/MTX type definitions needed to express the C versions, updated symbol references in include/functions.h and many asm files, adjusted ldscript.lcf, and cleaned missing trailing newlines. The PR body specifically notes a matching tactic: inline asm can be kept without breaking subsequent functions by re-enabling peephole optimization with a pragma because the asm keyword disables it.
what_changed: Deleted asm/aobj.s, removing 1476 lines of assembly, and added src/sysdolphin/aobj.c with 1385 lines of C and inline assembly.
Added C stubs or matched functions for src/sysdolphin/displayfunc.c, src/sysdolphin/fobj.c, and src/sysdolphin/wobj.c while trimming corresponding displayfunc_asm, fobj_asm, and wobj_asm assembly.
Added headers for aobj, fobj, wobj, displayfunc, jobj, video, initialize, and list to support typed C definitions in sysdolphin.
Added include/dolphin/gx/gxtypes.h and adjusted include/dolphin/mtx/mtxtypes.h, indicating type work was needed before matching the C code cleanly.
Updated include/functions.h with symbol/function declaration changes tied to newly matched functions.
Touched many remaining assembly files, likely to keep labels, references, or ordering consistent after moving aobj code into C.
Updated ldscript.lcf and removed an extra blank line from the Makefile; several one-line file changes were described as adding missing final newlines.
smart_moves: Converted a whole assembly file, asm/aobj.s, into a C translation unit rather than only matching isolated functions, giving future work a central typed implementation for AObj behavior.
Used force_active pragmas for an unused HSD_AObjGetAllocData function that still needed to exist for matching until it is referenced through an allocation-data function table.
Preserved matching progress with inline asm where necessary instead of blocking the PR on fully decompiled expressions.
Documented an important compiler-behavior workaround: re-enable peephole optimization with a pragma after inline asm because the asm keyword disables it and can affect following functions.
Added the supporting headers and Dolphin type definitions in the same PR, reducing reliance on untyped externs and making surrounding sysdolphin files easier to decompile.
decomp_lessons: When inline asm is used inside a C file, verify that compiler pragmas restore expected optimization behavior for subsequent functions; otherwise unrelated functions may stop matching.
Some unused but required functions may need #pragma force_active on to remain emitted until their eventual table reference or callsite is decompiled.
Large asm-to-C moves often require simultaneous header/type work; adding struct and type declarations can be as important as the function body itself.
For sysdolphin object modules, matching AObj code depends on typed relationships with FObj, JObj, object allocation, linked lists, and callback/global state.
Small formatting and EOF-newline cleanups can appear across many files during matching work; keep them separated in review notes when possible so they do not obscure actual matching changes.
Evidence for exact matching status of every function is limited in the slice, but the PR title/body explicitly claims several functions matched and aobj.c brought in all asm.
naming_conventions: Use public HSD-style names for identified sysdolphin functions, such as HSD_AObjInitAllocData, HSD_AObjGetAllocData, HSD_AObjGetFlags, HSD_AObjSetFlags, HSD_AObjClearFlags, HSD_AObjSetFObj, HSD_AObjInitEndCallBack, HSD_AObjInvokeCallBacks, HSD_AObjReqAnim, HSD_AObjStopAnim, and HSD_AObjInterpretAnim.
Use module-prefixed headers and source paths under src/sysdolphin for matched HAL/SysDolphin object systems: aobj, fobj, wobj, displayfunc, jobj.
Where symbols were not yet identified, the PR retained lbl_ and func_ address-derived names such as lbl_804C0880, lbl_804D7628, lbl_804D762C, lbl_804D7630, and jtbl_80405FEC.
A comment in aobj.c suggests documenting known data names next to raw labels, e.g. lbl_804C0880 as aobj_alloc_data and lbl_804DE438 as 0.0F.
AObj flag names appear as uppercase bitmask constants such as AOBJ_LOOP, AOBJ_NO_UPDATE, AOBJ_FIRST_PLAY, and AOBJ_NO_ANIM.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inline asm was deliberately used in C to bring over all of aobj.s while preserving matching where pure C was not yet viable.
The PR body highlights using a pragma to turn peephole optimization back on after inline asm because the asm keyword disables it and can affect subsequent functions.
Used #pragma push / #pragma force_active on / #pragma pop around HSD_AObjGetAllocData so the compiler emits an otherwise unused function required for current matching.
Moved a large contiguous assembly file to C while updating the linker script and adjacent assembly references, a tactic useful when object layout and text ordering must remain stable.
Kept raw extern labels for globals, constants, jump tables, and SDA data while gradually replacing function bodies with named C functions.

review_feedback.author: RevoSucks
review_feedback.lesson: Even matching-heavy inline-asm C files were expected to maintain basic formatting; the excerpt shows an indentation issue before an if statement in HSD_AObjInterpretAnim.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove inline asm from src/sysdolphin/aobj.c or rename remaining func_80364xxx/lbl_ symbols.
Search for later use of HSD_AObjGetAllocData in allocation-data function tables to see whether force_active remained necessary.
Compare later sysdolphin matching PRs for reuse of the peephole pragma workaround after inline asm.
Inspect later changes to aobj.h, fobj.h, wobj.h, and jobj.h for struct layout corrections introduced after this initial header pass.
Search review comments on formatting in early matching PRs to identify project style expectations for mixed C/inline-asm files.
